name: duplicate_signal_definition_in_scope
description: Code should include no duplicate definitions of the same signal name at the same scope level, whether via reg/wire declarations or initial/always procedural blocks. A signal must be defined exactly once per scope to avoid Verilator duplicate-definition errors.

name: ifdef_initial_then_reg_definition
description: Code should include no case where a signal is initialized in an `ifdef block via initial statement and then re-declared as reg outside that block at the same scope level. This creates conflicting definitions that Verilator rejects.
snippet:
`ifdef VLOGTB_HIER
  initial begin genclock = 1; end
`endif
reg genclock = 1;  // Conflict: genclock also initialized above

name: auto_generated_testbench_clock_declaration
description: Code should include a bootstrap clock signal generated via a procedural initial block that is driven unconditionally at time zero. This is a common pattern in auto-generated testbenches where genclock drives simulation timing before the design clock takes over.
